Iron saturation [Mass Fraction] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated NT-proBNP, suggestive of possible heart failure or significant cardiac strain. Mildly high hematocrit, red cell distribution width, potassium, and alkaline phosphatase are noted. Low HDL cholesterol and low total CO₂ may indicate increased cardiovascular risk and possible metabolic acidosis. Other parameters, including renal and liver function, are within normal limits. Clinical correlation and further cardiac evaluation are recommended.

text.div: Provider Organization: St Matthews Health & Community Centre
